headline: Nasdaq Logs Longest Winning Streak Since 2021 as Investors Look Beyond War
summary: Oil prices have retreated.  The prospect of a peace deal that reopens the Strait of Hormuz has now powered the Nasdaq composite to its longest streak of daily gains since November 2021—a 10-session ascent that has lifted the tech-heavy index 14%.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average’s energy-hungry components have the blue-chip index lagging behind other major benchmarks, on Tuesday notching a 0.7% gain.

content.title: Nasdaq Logs Longest Winning Streak Since 2021 as Investors Look Beyond War
content.summary: Oil prices have retreated.  The prospect of a peace deal that reopens the Strait of Hormuz has now powered the Nasdaq composite to its longest streak of daily gains since November 2021—a 10-session ascent that has lifted the tech-heavy index 14%.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average’s energy-hungry components have the blue-chip index lagging behind other major benchmarks, on Tuesday notching a 0.7% gain.

headline: Bank of America’s Stock Traders Book Record Revenue Amid Volatility
summary: Bank of America’s equities trading business drove record-high revenue in the first quarter as geopolitical tensions and artificial intelligence-related concerns prompted intense stock market volatility.  The second-largest U.S. bank said equities sales and trading revenue of $2.8 billion was a record, up nearly 30% from $2.2 billion a year earlier, on heightened client activity.  Bank of America said its overall sales and trading revenue jumped 13% from a year ago to $6.4 billion, its highest in more than a decade.

content.title: Bank of America’s Stock Traders Book Record Revenue Amid Volatility
content.summary: Bank of America’s equities trading business drove record-high revenue in the first quarter as geopolitical tensions and artificial intelligence-related concerns prompted intense stock market volatility. Bank of America said its overall sales and trading revenue jumped 13% from a year ago to $6.4 billion, its highest in more than a decade.

headline: Pimco Buys All $400 Million of Bonds Sold by Blue Owl BDC
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Pacific Investment Management Co. bought all $400 million of bonds issued Monday by a Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit fund, according to people with knowledge of the matter.The purchase comes amid a selloff that’s pushed spreads on notes from similar funds to multiyear highs in recent weeks, fueled in part by concerns over lending standards and exposure to software companies vulnerable to disruption from AI. headline: Hedge Funds Pivot to Bearish Dollar Bets on US-Iran Talks Optimism
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Hedge funds are increasingly downbeat on the dollar as the prospect of renewed US-Iran talks and a possible peace deal sap the currency’s war-driven strength. Investors added to their bearish dollar trades this month through April 10, based on a proprietary trading model from Morgan Stanley.In the options market, so-called risk reversals on the Bloomberg dollar index show the premium to hedge against a stronger dollar compared with bets against a weaker greenback has narrowed this

headline: BlackRock Gets Dual Price Target Hikes From Goldman Sachs and Barclays: Is This Asset Management Giant Unstoppable?
summary: BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) stock garnered two price target increases on April 15, following a blowout first quarter that had Wall Street reassessing this asset management platform’s power. Barclays raised its price target to $1,310 from $1,290 and kept an Overweight rating, while Goldman Sachs analyst Alexander Blostein raised his target to $1,313 from $1,181 and maintained ... headline: Five Things We Learned From Bank Earnings
summary: The nation’s biggest banks this week painted a reassuring picture of the U.S. economy, saying consumers are still spending despite surging gas prices since the Iran war began.  Trading desks are raking in money from volatile markets and dealmakers are busier than they have been in a while.

content.title: Five Things We Learned From Bank Earnings
content.summary: The nation’s biggest banks this week painted a reassuring picture of the U.S. economy, saying consumers are still spending despite surging gas prices since the Iran war began.  Trading desks are raking in money from volatile markets and dealmakers are busier than they have been in a while.
